title: Train a graded imminence ranking sidecar over future pit distance and blend by OOF ranks
summary: Learning-to-rank docs from XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost support grouped pairwise/query objectives.
rationale: Learning-to-rank docs from XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost support grouped pairwise/query objectives. Instead of binary `PitNextLap` alone, create a relevance target such as {pit in 1 lap > 2 laps > 3 laps > no near stop} within each driver-race stint or race-lap query. This develops the prior event-time idea into a sharper, testable objective that is still compatible with ROC AUC via score blending.
implementation_hint: Label each row by distance-to-next-pit capped at K laps, then train a ranking model with groups like `Year-Race-Driver-Stint` or `Year-Race-LapNumber`. Use `rank_xendcg`, `rank:ndcg`, or CatBoost pairwise ranking. Blend its OOF raw scores with the main binary model using foldwise rank averaging or a monotone OOF blender, since ROC AUC accepts decision scores directly.
expected_effect: Improves ordering around the pit window even when exact next-lap timing is noisy, which should lift AUC more reliably than another hand-tuned heuristic block.
risk: Group design matters: poor grouping can dilute signal, and a ranking sidecar may underperform if many stints have only one informative positive neighborhood.
